name: Raspberry Honey-Almond Parfait
recipeIngredient: 1/2 each Vanilla bean, split the long way, seeds removed and reserved, OR
2 oz Almonds, sliced (about 2/3 cups), toasted (350°F for 10 minutes), cooled
30 oz Raspberries, frozen, thawed and drained
1/3 cup Egg whites (slightly more than the whites of two large eggs)
2 tbsp Framboise (raspberry brandy) or 2 tbsp Raspberry liqueur
30 oz Raspberries, frozen, thawed and drained
recipeInstructions:
For Honey-Almond Parfait:
1. Place the outer ring of an 8-inch springform pan on a flat serving plate; put it in the freezer.
2. Whip 1/2 cup of cream until stiff; refrigerate.
3. In the top of a double boiler or mixer bowl that fits snugly into a saucepan, whisk together egg, egg yolk, honey and vanilla seeds. Place this container over a pan of boiling water and whisk vigorously until lightly thickened (5-7 minutes).
4. Remove from heat and continue beating until thick and completely cooled.
5. Fold in the almonds and reserved whipped cream. Pour the mixture into the chilled springform mold. Dip a finger in cold water and run it around the top rim of the mixture, forming a clean edge. Return it all to the freezer.
For Raspberry Parfait:
1. Coarsely mash 14 ounces of raspberries in a food mill, processor or with the back of a large wooden spoon.
2. Place the fruit in a saucepan with 1/3 cup of sugar and bring the mixture to a boil. Boil gently, stirring often, for 5 minutes. Strain the cooked fruit mixture to eliminate any seeds. Transfer to a shallow bowl and cool in the freezer or refrigerate.
3. Meanwhile whip 1 cup of cream until stiff; refrigerate.
4. Beat egg whites until they form soft peaks; then gradually add 1/2 cup sugar and continue to beat until stiff and shiny.
5. Partially fold in the cooked raspberry puree, framboise, and whipped cream and gently fold until blended. Pour the mixture over the honey-almond layer in the springform mold, smoothing the top with a spatula. Freeze for several hours or overnight.
For Raspberry Sauce:
1. In a saucepan, combine all of the sauce ingredients except lemon juice. Bring to a boil, then boil gently, stirring, for about 7 minutes. Reduce the heat and stir in the lemon juice. Keep warm.
2. To garnish, remove parfait from freezer. Carefully release springform ring (if difficult, warm ring slightly with your palms).
3. Whip 1/2 cup cream until stiff; form a decorative border or rosettes around the top and bottom edges of the parfait by piping whipped cream through a pastry bag fitted with a star tip.
4. Arrange 2 raspberries and 2-3 almond slices on top of each serving. Cut into wedges. Serve with warm raspberry sauce, if desired.
